# SeqLPD: Sequence Matching Enhanced Loop-Closure Detection Based on Large-Scale Point Cloud Description for Self-Driving Vehicles

## Accepted structured facts

- **datasets:** Oxford RobotCar, KITTI, Chinese University of Hong Kong campus vehicle dataset — `source_fact`; manual, p. 4–6, § VI-A. RobotCar;VI-B. KITTI;VI-C. Real Experimental Results, Fig. 5-8;Table I-II
- **input_unit:** sequence of 3D point-cloud submaps, Oxford submap covers about 20 m of vehicle trajectory and contains 4096 normalized points — `source_fact`; manual, p. 2–4, § III. System Framework;VI-A. Place Recognition Results in RobotCar Datasets, Fig. 1
- **invariances:** point-order permutation — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 2–5, § II. Related Work;IV-A. Local Feature Extraction;IV-B. Graph-Based Feature Neighborhood Aggregation;VI-C. Real Experimental Results, Fig. 3-4;Fig. 6
- **representation:** LPD-Light local geometric features, graph-based feature aggregation, NetVLAD 256-D global descriptor, K-means++ descriptor clusters, super keyframes, coarse matching and SeqSLAM-style local sequence matching — `source_fact`; manual, p. 2–4, § III. System Framework;IV. Large-Scale Place Description;V. Loop-Closure Detection, Fig. 1-4
- **uses_learning:** yes — `source_fact`; manual, p. 1–2, § Abstract;IV. Large-Scale Place Description, Fig. 2
- **uses_sequence:** yes — `source_fact`; manual, p. 1–4, § Abstract;III. System Framework;V-C. Coarse Matching and Local Sequence Matching, Fig. 1;Fig. 6
